title: Prompt Injection
description: Crafted prompts manipulate the model to ignore its instructions, exfiltrate data, or escalate privileges. Direct (user input) and indirect (retrieved content) variants.

title: Insecure Output Handling
description: Downstream systems treat LLM output as trusted (XSS, SQLi, RCE) when it could contain attacker-controlled payloads.
mitigations: treat LLM output as untrusted by default

title: Training Data Poisoning
description: Adversaries inject data into training/fine-tuning sets to introduce backdoors or bias.

title: Model Denial of Service
description: Crafted inputs cause disproportionate resource use (long context, recursive tool calls, expensive generations).

title: Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
description: Third-party models, datasets, or libraries introduce risk (compromised weights, malicious plugins, vulnerable deps).

title: Sensitive Information Disclosure
description: Model leaks PII, secrets, or proprietary content learned in training or context.

title: Insecure Plugin Design
description: Plugins/tools accept untrusted parameters or grant excessive scope.

title: Excessive Agency
description: Agent loops have too much autonomy (too many tools, no human-check on sensitive actions, unbounded loops).

title: Overreliance
description: Users trust model outputs without verification — hallucinated facts, incorrect code, biased recommendations.

title: Model Theft
description: Unauthorized exfiltration of model weights or systematic extraction via API queries.
